Primary results: child diet and metabolites

Child diet is related to child serum metabolites at 2 years, 5-6 years, and across the repeated 2-year to 5-6-year follow-up window. The repeated-measures component should be interpreted more cautiously than the linear-model components. For multi-level diet-quality class exposures in the linear-model tables below, the reported P and Q values come from omnibus factor tests across class levels, so no single beta or confidence interval is shown for those rows.

1 Signal summary

This table shows how much result volume and FDR-significant signal each analysis component contributes by exposure family before the detailed tables below.

Show code 
datatable(
  signal_summary_tbl,
  caption = "Question-level signal summary",
  options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = FALSE, autoWidth = FALSE)
)

2 Concurrent associations at 2 years

Visit-6 analytical population. Complete-case n across fitted models: 189-219.

Nutrients

Show code 
show_results_table(filter_family(q1, "Nutrients"), "Associations at 2 years (visit 6) - Nutrients")

Diet Quality

Show code 
show_results_table(filter_family(q1, "Diet Quality"), "Associations at 2 years (visit 6) - Diet Quality")

4 Prospective associations: 2-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites

Prospective analytical population. Complete-case n across fitted models: 77-107. Each visit-7 metabolite is conditioned on its own visit-6 baseline value.

Nutrients

Show code 
show_results_table(filter_family(q3, "Nutrients"), "Prospective associations (2-year diet -> 5-6-year metabolites) - Nutrients")

Diet Quality

Show code 
show_results_table(filter_family(q3, "Diet Quality"), "Prospective associations (2-year diet -> 5-6-year metabolites) - Diet Quality")

5 Repeated-measures associations across 2 years and 5-6 years

The repeated-measures model uses the paired visit-6 and visit-7 diet constructs within child and reports both the pooled diet main effect and the diet-by-visit interaction.

These estimates should be treated more cautiously than Q1-Q3. In the executed analysis, this model family produced singular-fit and rank-deficiency warnings. The saved results object does not currently encode warning counts, so the tables below report coefficient output, not clean-fit certification.

Diet main-effect rows. Complete-case n across fitted models: 199-335.

Nutrients

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_main, "Nutrients"), "Repeated-measures main effects - Nutrients")

Diet Quality

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_main, "Diet Quality"), "Repeated-measures main effects - Diet Quality")

Diet-by-visit interaction rows. Complete-case n across fitted models: 199-335.

Nutrients

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_interaction, "Nutrients"), "Repeated-measures diet-by-visit interaction effects - Nutrients")

Diet Quality

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_interaction, "Diet Quality"), "Repeated-measures diet-by-visit interaction effects - Diet Quality")

6 Top associations from the concurrent and prospective models

This summary intentionally excludes Q4 because the repeated-measures outputs have a different structure and a heavier model-warning burden.

Show code 
all_res <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% mutate(analysis = "Concurrent: 2-year diet and 2-year metabolites"),
  q2 %>% mutate(analysis = "Concurrent: 5-6-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites"),
  q3 %>% mutate(analysis = "Prospective: 2-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites")
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(q.value)) %>%
  arrange(q.value)

top_hits <- all_res %>% slice_head(n = 20)

ggplot(top_hits, aes(x = estimate, y = reorder(paste(analysis, exposure, feature, sep = " | "), -q.value), color = analysis)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high), height = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(x = "Beta (SD units per unit exposure)", y = "Analysis | Exposure | Metabolite", title = "Top 20 significant associations") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)

7 Repeated-measures supporting tables

These highlight tables are descriptive rankings of the saved Q4 coefficient rows. They should not be read as evidence that the mixed-model family was numerically clean.

Main Effects: Nutrients

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_main_top, "Nutrients"), "Top repeated-measures main-effect terms - Nutrients")

Main Effects: Diet Quality

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_main_top, "Diet Quality"), "Top repeated-measures main-effect terms - Diet Quality")

Interactions: Nutrients

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_interaction_top, "Nutrients"), "Top repeated-measures interaction terms - Nutrients")

Interactions: Diet Quality

Show code 
show_mixed_table(filter_family(q4_interaction_top, "Diet Quality"), "Top repeated-measures interaction terms - Diet Quality")

8 Sensitivity summary

Show code 
comp_q1 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q1, q1_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q1 concurrent")
comp_q2 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q2, q2_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q2 concurrent")
comp_q3 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q3, q3_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit_exposure", "visit_outcome")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q3 prospective")
comp_q4 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q4, q4_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q4 repeated")

comp_all <- bind_rows(comp_q1, comp_q2, comp_q3, comp_q4)

if (nrow(comp_all) > 0) {
  summary_tbl <- comp_all %>%
    mutate(exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure)) %>%
    group_by(analysis, exposure_family, exposure) %>%
    summarise(
      pairs_compared = n(),
      median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      p90_abs_delta = as.numeric(quantile(abs_delta, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
      direction_flip_fraction = mean(direction_flip, na.rm = TRUE),
      q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
      p90_abs_delta = round(p90_abs_delta, 3),
      direction_flip_fraction = round(direction_flip_fraction, 3),
      q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3)
    )

  datatable(
    summary_tbl,
    caption = "Primary-vs-sensitivity estimate stability",
    filter = "top",
    options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = FALSE, autoWidth = FALSE)
  )
}

9 Execution status

This status table records execution status only. ok means an exposure-question combination returned saved output rows; it does not mean the corresponding models were free of singular fits, rank deficiency, or other estimation warnings.

Show code 
show_status_table(model_status, "Executed model status summary")
